pr.title: Improve matching in Melee source files
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Large batch matching PR across 57 src/melee files, spanning effect, fighter, game-mode, stage, interface, menu, map, toy, library, and player bonus code. The PR body reports require-protos and ninja changes_all validation, matched code/data gains, 13 new matches in the author's saved-baseline report, 0 broken matches, 123 unmatched-item improvements, and 0 unmatched-item regressions. Review feedback focused on avoiding pragmas, replacing repeated generated-looking blocks with loops, removing M2C_FIELD where possible, mapping jobj.h asserts back to HSD_JObj-style helpers, and reverting fake/regressive HSD_JObj changes.

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: new matches table and unmatched improvements table

stage matching work; PR body reports large function improvement and .rodata/.sdata new matches; review summary records loop feedback
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: grKongo_801D7134 improved from 0.27% to 95.13%; grKongo .rodata and .sdata reached 100.00%

what_changed: Changed 57 files under src/melee, with 7984 added lines and 7198 deleted lines according to PR metadata.

PR body says the author fixed regressions reported by saved-baseline comparison and added a strict local regression gate so changes/changes_all fail when any unit, section, or function metric moves backward.
PR body reports validation on a clean PR worktree at ff551b3 against baseline dae3629 using python3 configure.py --require-protos and ninja changes_all.

Author's saved-baseline report lists matched code at 70.39% (+0.24%, +9176 bytes), matched data at 41.33% (+0.06%, +712 bytes), 13 new matches, 0 broken matches, 123 improvements in unmatched items, and 0 regressions in unmatched items.

A bot issue comment reports a similar GALE01 comparison for 83229e3 - a9e6faa with matched code 70.52% (+0.23%, +8872 bytes), matched data 41.33% (+0.06%, +696 bytes), 11 new matches, and 122 unmatched improvements.
Diff excerpts show repeated effect changes in efasync.c and efsync.c where a computed half-pi/rotation value is copied to a temporary before HSD_JObjSetRotationY.
Diff excerpts show ftCo_8009F834 replacing separate float locals with Vec3 locals and simplifying multiple efAsync_Spawn calls by removing extra vararg-looking arguments.
Diff excerpts show ftCo_800A2718 expanded from inline helper composition into explicit item iteration and stage switch logic, bracketed by dont_inline pragmas.

Review comments indicate some submitted shapes were still review-sensitive: pragmas, unrolled/repeated code, M2C_FIELD use, jobj.h assert expansions, and a likely fake/regressive HSD_JObj-related change.

smart_moves.text: Kept validation tied to a saved-baseline report and named the exact local commands used: require-protos and changes_all.
Reported both match gains and zero regressions/broken matches, making the reviewable claim about no backward movement explicit.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 0 broken matches and 0 regressions sections

smart_moves.text: Converted several separate x/y/z float local groups in ftCo_8009F834 into Vec3 locals where the diff shows whole-vector copy and component updates.

In gmresult.c, introduced an explicit ResultsPlayerData pointer and a for loop over four players for part of fn_80175DC8 setup, improving structure over the prior best-effort placeholder implementation.
evidence_refs: review_comments: gmresult.c hunk around pdata = lbl_8046DBE8.player_data; for (i = 0; i < 4; i++, pdata++)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Repeated blocks that walk parallel player/flag slots are strong candidates for a loop, even if an initial pointer-increment form improves match score.
Large JObj setup blocks with repeated lb_80011E24 calls should be checked for natural loops or table-driven patterns before preserving many switch/repeated cases.
M2C_FIELD should be treated as bridge code; reviewers explicitly asked to remove it where possible in gmresult matching work.
Expanded jobj.h asserts are review evidence to look for the owning HSD_JObj helper or included jobj function rather than leaving raw assert-expanded code.
Pragmas such as global_optimizer off or dont_inline are review-sensitive matching tactics and should be avoided or tightly justified by verifier evidence.
Reviewer feedback can identify fake-match risk even when a change may improve local matching; in lb_00F9.c the reviewer called an HSD_JObj-related change likely regressive/fake and said to undo it.

naming_conventions.name: fp, ip, gobj, jobj
naming_conventions.convention: Short role names for clear Fighter, Item, GObj, and JObj locals are consistent with the loaded conservative-naming standard and appear in the diff excerpts.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800A2718 cur_ip and gobj/jobj uses
raw_diff: efasync/efsync jobj_1 and jobj_2 uses

naming_conventions.name: address-style or unknown labels
naming_conventions.convention: Unproven data/function meanings remain address-style, such as lbl_803B7B18 and fn_80175DC8 in the gmresult excerpt.
evidence_refs: review_comments: gmresult.c hunk showing lbl_803B7B18 and fn_80175DC8

naming_conventions.name: spXX locals
naming_conventions.convention: Stack-shape-driven locals such as sp108, spFC, sp84, and spA8 appear in matching-heavy excerpts; this is evidence of match-oriented temporary naming, not a general semantic naming standard.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use exact validation ledgers for broad matching PRs: require-protos plus changes_all, with saved-baseline before/after metrics and explicit no-regression reporting.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Validation and Expected/local run sections

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used in several excerpts to influence stack shape, including gmresult and ftCo_0A01; this remains a tactic requiring narrow evidence rather than a style lesson.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800A229C PAD_STACK(8), ftCo_800A2718 PAD_STACK(6 * 4), ftCo_800A3908 PAD_STACK(0x20)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Temporary float copies before HSD_JObjSetRotationY were used in effect spawn/dispatch functions to alter codegen.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: efasync.c f32_1 = rot_y before HSD_JObjSetRotationY
raw_diff: efsync.c va_f32_1 = half_pi before HSD_JObjSetRotationY

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Whole-Vec3 locals can replace adjacent scalar stack locals when the source operation is a vector copy followed by component perturbation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_09F7.c spA8 = *arg5 and sp84 = *arg5 changes

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Do not treat pragmas as ordinary matching cleanup; review explicitly objected to pragma use in gm_1601.c.

review_feedback.feedback: Repeated player/flag code in fn_80161154 is most likely a for loop, including similar repeated code below it.
A repeated setup block in fn_80175DC8 is probably a for loop; out-of-order 6/7 should still be checked.
Try to remove any M2C_FIELD if possible.
Any jobj.h asserts are indications of an included/run jobj.c function.
Undo the change; reviewer called it likely a regression/fake match and said the HSD_JObj form was likely how it was written.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, merged state, changed file counts, review feedback examples
human_pr_text: PR body, validation commands, saved-baseline results
review_comments: loaded review comments excerpt, truncated after gmresult section
raw_diff: loaded raw diff excerpt, truncated after ftCo_0A01 excerpt
notes: Changed-file metadata is complete for 57 files, but raw diff and review comments are truncated, so file-specific extraction is strongest for files visible in excerpts.
The PR body reports validation and match metrics, and a bot comment reports similar metrics, but this intake did not independently run builds, objdiff, or regression tools.

Review feedback examples include tools/changes_fmt.py, but that path does not appear in the supplied changed_files list; intake records the review feedback without claiming changed-file status for that path.
Some tactics visible in excerpts, such as PAD_STACK, pointer casts, temporary float copies, and pragmas, are recorded as matching tactics rather than accepted source style.

The PR was merged according to metadata, but individual reviewer suggestions may or may not have been fully addressed in the final merged diff; this intake does not infer resolution beyond supplied evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Review repeated generated-looking blocks as loops before preserving pointer-step/unrolled forms
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2581, reviewer feedback on gm_1601.c and gmresult.c called out repeated player/flag and JObj setup blocks as likely for loops. This supports the existing natural-loops standard for matching work: repeated blocks varying by index, pointer, flag slot, or stride should be checked as for/while/do-while forms before keeping generated-looking repetition.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Treat pragmas as exceptional matching tactics, not default cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: Reviewer feedback on gm_1601.c explicitly said to avoid pragmas if possible. PR #2581 also shows dont_inline/global_optimizer pragma usage in matching excerpts, making this a useful historical example for the avoid-pragmas-register-asm and matching-tactics-need-evidence standards.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer typed fields/helpers over M2C_FIELD and raw generated access where recoverable
accepted_candidate_records.text: Reviewer feedback on gmresult.c asked to remove any M2C_FIELD if possible, aligning with the accepted standard that M2C_FIELD is bridge code while recovering real fields or helper accesses.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Map jobj.h assert expansions back to HSD_JObj operations when local evidence supports it
accepted_candidate_records.text: Review feedback in PR #2581 said jobj.h asserts indicate an included/run jobj.c function. This is graph-safe as a PR-specific reinforcement of the loaded header-inlines standard, not a new global rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting strict saved-baseline regression gates for changes/changes_all
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2581 body says it added a strict local regression gate so changes/changes_all fail when any unit, section, or function metric moves backward. Curator may want to route this to tooling docs or a changes_fmt/changes_all source note if supported by the actual tooling diff.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR body supports the existence of a tooling/regression-gate change, but the loaded changed_files list does not include tools/changes_fmt.py and the relevant tooling diff is not loaded. This should be curator-reviewed before any source-corpus update.

source_update_candidates.title: Potential historical example for fake-match rejection around HSD_JObj forms
source_update_candidates.text: Reviewer feedback on lb_00F9.c said to undo a change as likely regression/fake match and that the HSD_JObj form was likely how it was written. This may be useful as an example under infer-authored-source-style or header-inlines, but should not become a new standalone rule without the full diff and final resolution.
source_update_candidates.reason: Evidence is a review-feedback example without the full local hunk in loaded excerpts; curator should inspect the full review/diff and final merged state before updating standards.

rejection_notes: Do not promote PAD_STACK, temporary float copies before HSD_JObjSetRotationY, pointer casts into efLib_AnimQueue, or dont_inline/global_optimizer pragmas as accepted source style; the evidence only supports them as match tactics seen in this PR, and standards require explicit verifier evidence.

Do not claim every changed file improved or matched; the PR body lists selected new matches and the top 30 of 123 unmatched improvements, while many file-specific outcomes are not visible in the loaded excerpt.

Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the literal comments; the review comments suggest loops, pragma avoidance, M2C_FIELD cleanup, jobj.h helper recovery, and a fake-match concern, but final resolution is not proven by the slice.
Do not add tools/changes_fmt.py as a changed file in this postmortem; it appears only in review feedback examples, not in the supplied changed_files list.
